$comment: AFFILIATE RULES — maintained by the DEVELOPER, not by Caitlin. She never sees this file.
The enrich Action reads this to turn a raw retailer URL into Caitlin's commissionable link.
TWO SEPARATE RAILS. ShopMy does not monetise Amazon at all (their words:
'we do not offer commissions on Amazon products directly'). So Amazon needs its own
Associates tag no matter what, and Amazon URLs must NEVER be wrapped in ShopMy —
that would make the referring site ambiguous to Amazon, which is a cloaking violation.
HARD RULE FROM AMAZON'S OPERATING AGREEMENT: no link shorteners, no self-hosted
redirects (/go/product), no cloaking. Links must be direct <a href> on a public page.
Click tracking is therefore client-side only. Do not 'improve' this by adding a redirect.

accounts.$comment: FILL THESE IN once Caitlin is approved. Until then, products publish unmonetised and get flagged in /admin.

amazon.note: Exactly what Amazon's own SiteStripe does. Compliant. Cookie is only 24h — send look pages the same day.

shopmy.note: ShopMy's documented wrap-any-link format. Only applied to retailers we know are in their network.

fallback.note: Unknown domain: keep the raw link so the card still works, but mark status=needs-attention so Caitlin sees in /admin that it is NOT earning. Never silently pretend a link is monetised.
